<summary>
  <request>Create automated BI data extraction routine for monthly metrics including MOL, shipments, and air vs sea distribution from Qlik Sense dashboard</request>
  <investigated>The BI website was examined and identified as a Qlik Sense dashboard. The accessibility tree structure was analyzed to determine which data points are directly readable and what interaction patterns are needed for month selection.   <notes>The direct accessibility of numeric values through the DOM significantly simplifies the automation approach. The main technical challenge shifts from data extraction to understanding the filter selection UI pattern in Qlik Sense, which will enable month-specific data retrieval.</notes>
</summary>

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Qlik Sense Filter Interaction Pattern Identified</title>
  <subtitle>Filter panels open with searchable value lists and require explicit confirmation of selections</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Clicking filter headings in Qlik Sense opens modal filter panel with searchable value list</fact>
    <fact>Anno Competenza filter shows available years: 1970, 2002, 2024, 2025, 2026</fact>
    <fact>Filter panel provides search box for filtering results as user types</fact>
    <fact>Selection workflow requires explicit confirmation via "Conferma selezione" button after selecting values</fact>
    <fact>Filter panel includes "Cancella selezione" (clear), "Annulla selezione" (cancel), and "Altro" (more) actions</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The Qlik Sense filter interaction pattern was revealed by clicking on the "Anno Competenza" filter heading. The system opens a modal filter panel overlay containing a searchable list of available values. The panel displays 5 available years and includes a dynamic search box with placeholder text "Cerca nella casella di elenco" that filters results as the user types. The selection workflow follows a three-step pattern: open filter panel, select desired values from the list, then explicitly confirm the selection using the "Conferma selezione" button. This pattern is critical for automating data extraction since filter values must be programmatically selected and confirmed before the dashboard data updates to reflect the filtered view.</narrative>
  <concepts>
    <concept>how-it-works</concept>
    <concept>pattern</concept>
  </concepts>
  <files_read></files_read>
  <files_modified></files_modified>
</observation>

The user clicked on the 2026 row in the Anno Competenza filter. Looking at the outcome:

1. The row is now marked as "Selezionato" (Selected) and has an img/checkmark icon
2. The status changed from "Nessun valore selezionato" to "È presente un valore selezionato" (One value selected)
3. A filter pill/tag appeared showing "Anno Competenza: 2026" with a remove button
4. The dashboard data updated to show 2026-specific data:
   - Monthly labels changed to "2026-gen", "2026-feb", "2026-mar" (January, February, March 2026)
   - New comparison values appeared: "Ricavi 2025: 6.21M", "Costi 2025: 4.55M"
   - Traffic numbers updated: 273, 327, 120
   - Margin percentages updated: 22.2%, 30.6%, 23.5%
   - Range Data Competenza filter became visible

This is important because it shows:
1. How to select a value in the filter (click the row)
2. The visual feedback of selection (checkmark, "Selezionato" state)
3. That the filter is immediately reflected in the UI (pill appears)
4. But the data hasn't fully updated yet - they still need to confirm the selection

Actually, looking more carefully at the data, it seems the dashboard HAS updated with 2026 data - the monthly labels show "2026-gen", "2026-feb", "2026-mar". So it appears the selection might auto-apply without needing explicit confirmation, or there's a pending state.

This is a discovery about the filter selection mechanism and how the dashboard responds to filter changes.

<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Qlik Sense Filter Selection Updates Dashboard in Real-Time</title>
  <subtitle>Selecting year 2026 immediately updates all dashboard visualizations and adds filter tag to toolbar</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Clicking filter row marks it as "Selezionato" state with checkmark icon and updates status to "È presente un valore selezionato"</fact>
    <fact>Selected filter creates pill tag in toolbar showing "Anno Competenza: 2026" with remove button</fact>
    <fact>Dashboard data updates immediately to reflect 2026 filter including monthly labels "2026-gen", "2026-feb", "2026-mar"</fact>
    <fact>Traffic chart updates to show 2026 shipment counts: 273 (January), 327 (February), 120 (March)</fact>
    <fact>Margin percentages recalculated for 2026 data: 22.2%, 30.6%, 23.5% across months</fact>
    <fact>Comparison metrics appear showing prior year context: "Ricavi 2025: 6.21M", "Costi 2025: 4.55M"</fact>
    <fact>Range Data Competenza filter becomes visible after year selection</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Selecting the 2026 year filter demonstrates that Qlik Sense updates dashboard visualizations immediately upon row selection, without requiring explicit confirmation. The selection adds a removable filter pill to the toolbar displaying "Anno Competenza: 2026", and all dashboard components refresh to show year-specific data. The traffic chart updates monthly labels to "2026-gen/feb/mar" format with corresponding shipment counts (273, 327, 120), margin percentages recalculate (22.2%, 30.6%, 23.5%), and the system adds prior year comparison metrics (Ricavi 2025: 6.21M, Costi 2025: 4.55M) for context. Additionally, selecting a year filter reveals the "Range Data Competenza" date range filter, suggesting a hierarchical filter dependency where year selection enables more granular date filtering. This real-time update behavior simplifies automation since filter changes don't require waiting for explicit confirmation actions.</narrative>

2. The dashboard is now showing the complete 2026 data
3. All metrics are visible with 2026 values:
   - Ricavi 2026: 1.52M (vs Ricavi 2025: 6.21M)
   - Costi 2026: 1.16M (vs Costi 2025: 4.55M)
   - Saldo 2026: 363.7k (vs Saldo 2025: 1.66M)
   - Saldo % 2026: 23.9% (vs Saldo % 2025: 26.7%)
4. Incidenza (Distribution) charts show:
   - Ricavi: Aereo 84.6%, Marittimo 15.4%
   - Costi: Aereo 84.9%, Marittimo 15.1%

6. The filter pill remains in the toolbar showing "Anno Competenza: 2026"

This confirms the full workflow: click filter → select value → confirm → panel closes and data is finalized.

content.thinking:
The user clicked on "Mese Competenza" (Month filter) and the filter panel opened. Looking at the outcome:

1. The filter panel shows Italian month abbreviations: gen, feb, mar, apr, mag, giu, lug, ago, set, ott, nov, dic
2. Some months are marked as "Opzionale" (Optional) - gen, feb, mar - these are the months with data in 2026
3. Other months are marked as "Escluso" (Excluded) - apr through dic - these months don't have data yet in 2026 (it's currently March 2026)
4. Status shows "Nessun valore selezionato" (No value selected)
5. Has the same control buttons: Altro, Cancella selezione, Annulla selezione, Conferma selezione
6. Has a search box to filter month names

This is important because it shows:
1. The month filter is contextual - it only shows available months based on the year filter
2. Qlik Sense marks unavailable values as "Escluso" 
3. Available months are "Opzionale"
4. This demonstrates the filter dependency/hierarchy - year selection affects which months are available

This is a discovery about the month filter structure and how Qlik Sense handles contextual filtering.

content.text:
<observation>
  <type>discovery</type>
  <title>Month Filter Shows Contextual Values Based on Year Selection</title>
  <subtitle>Mese Competenza filter displays Italian month abbreviations with availability status reflecting 2026 YTD data range</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Month filter displays Italian abbreviations: gen, feb, mar, apr, mag, giu, lug, ago, set, ott, nov, dic</fact>
    <fact>Available months marked as "Opzionale" include gen, feb, mar corresponding to YTD 2026 data range</fact>
    <fact>Future months marked as "Escluso" (Excluded) include apr through dic indicating no data available yet for 2026</fact>
    <fact>Filter panel shows "Nessun valore selezionato" indicating no month-specific filter currently applied</fact>
    <fact>Month filter is contextually dependent on year filter displaying only relevant months for selected year</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>Opening the "Mese Competenza" (Month) filter reveals Qlik Sense's contextual filtering behavior where available filter values depend on parent filter selections. The month list displays Italian three-letter abbreviations (gen through dic) with two distinct states: "Opzionale" for months with available data (January, February, March) and "Escluso" for months without data (April through December). This contextual filtering reflects the Year-to-Date 2026 reality where only the first three months have data. The hierarchical filter dependency means the automation workflow must apply year filters before month filters to ensure valid month selections. The filter maintains the same interaction pattern with search capability and explicit confirmation buttons, allowing programmatic selection of specific months to drill down from yearly to monthly data granularity for extracting month-specific MOL, shipment counts, and air vs sea distribution metrics.</narrative>

Prendi uno snapshot con `mcp__plugin_playwright_playwright__browser_snapshot` per trovare il ref del heading \\\"Anno Competenza\\\"\\n2. Clicca su \\\"Anno Competenza\\\" heading\\n3. Clicca il pulsante \\\"Conferma selezione\\\" (testid: `actions-toolbar-confirm`)\\n\\n### Step 5 — Seleziona Mese\\n1. Prendi uno snapshot per trovare il ref del heading \\\"Mese Competenza\\\"\\n2. Clicca il pulsante \\\"Conferma selezione\\\" (testid: `actions-toolbar-confirm`)\\n\\n### Step 6 — Leggi i dati\\nPrendi uno snapshot finale. I dati sono leggibili direttamente dall'accessibility tree negli elementi `application`:\\n- **Ricavi**: cerca `KPI Nessun titolo Ricavi [anno] (DP) [valore]`\\n- **Costi**: cerca `KPI Nessun titolo Costi [anno] (DP) [valore]`\\n- **Saldo/MOL**: cerca `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo [anno] (DP) [valore]`\\n- **Saldo %/MOL%**: cerca `KPI Nessun titolo Saldo % [anno] [valore]`\\n- **Incidenza Ricavi**: cerca `Grafico a torta Incidenza Ricavi Aereo [x]% Marittimo [y]%`\\n- **Incidenza Saldo**: cerca `Grafico a torta Incidenza Saldo Aereo [x]% Marittimo [y]%`\\n- **Traffico/Spedizioni**: cerca `Grafico a barre Traffico` — i numeri dopo le etichette mese sono le spedizioni\\n\\n### Step 7 — Formatta e presenta\\nPresenta i dati in questo formato:\\n\\n```\\n## BI Report — [Mese] [Anno]\\n\\n### KPI Principali\\n| Indicatore | Valore |\\n|------------|--------|\\n| Ricavi | X,XXM |\\n| Costi | X,XXM |\\n| MOL (Saldo) | XXX,Xk |\\n| MOL% | XX,X% |\\n\\n### Mix Aereo / Marittimo\\n| Metrica | Aereo | Marittimo |\\n|---------|-------|-----------|\\n| Ricavi | XX.X% | XX.X% |\\n| Saldo | XX.X% | XX.X% |\\n\\n### Traffico\\n| Mese | Spedizioni |\\n|------|------------|\\n| [mese] | XXX |\\n```\\n\\n## Note tecniche\\n- Qlik Sense è lento a caricarsi: se il snapshot mostra solo `img` senza testo, aspetta qualche secondo e riprova con un altro snapshot\\n- Dopo il login, la sessione rimane attiva per la durata della conversazione\\n- Se il mese richiesto risulta \\\"Escluso\\\" nel filtro, significa che non ci sono dati per quel mese/anno — avvisare Roy\\n- Se già loggato dalla sessione corrente, salta Step 1-2 e vai direttamente a Step 3\\n\"}"</parameters>

<observation>
  <type>feature</type>
  <title>Complete BI Data Extraction Skill Implementation</title>
  <subtitle>Documented end-to-end automation workflow for extracting monthly business metrics from Qlik Sense dashboard via /dati-bi command</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Skill invoked via `/dati-bi [mese] [anno]` command accepting Italian month names and 4-digit years</fact>
    <fact>Complete month mapping table converts Italian names to Qlik abbreviations (gennaio→gen through dicembre→dic)</fact>
    <fact>Authentication workflow documented with login URL, dashboard URL, username cltsara, password sara2026</fact>
    <fact>Seven-step procedure: navigate to login, authenticate, navigate to dashboard, select year filter, select month filter, extract data, format report</fact>
    <fact>Data extraction patterns specified for each metric type using accessibility tree selectors</fact>
    <fact>KPI extraction patterns: Ricavi, Costi, Saldo (MOL), Saldo % using KPI application elements</fact>
    <fact>Distribution extraction patterns: Incidenza Ricavi and Incidenza Saldo from pie chart applications showing Aereo vs Marittimo percentages</fact>
    <fact>Traffic extraction pattern: shipment counts from Grafico a barre Traffico bar chart application</fact>
    <fact>Structured output format defined with three tables: KPI Principali, Mix Aereo/Marittimo, Traffico</fact>
    <fact>Technical notes handle edge cases: loading delays, session persistence, excluded month detection</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The complete BI data extraction skill was implemented as a documented workflow in /Users/royrigamonti/Chuck/.claude/skills/dati-bi/SKILL.md. The skill provides a command-line interface (`/dati-bi [mese] [anno]`) for extracting monthly business intelligence metrics from the Qlik Sense dashboard at beoneanalytics.novasystems.it. The implementation documents a seven-step automation process: authenticate with credentials (cltsara/sara2026), navigate to the dashboard, apply year and month filters using the discovered click-select-confirm pattern, extract data from the accessibility tree using specific application element patterns, and format results into structured tables. Data extraction patterns target KPI elements for financial metrics (Ricavi, Costi, Saldo/MOL with values and percentages), pie chart elements for transportation mode distributions (Aereo vs Marittimo percentages across revenue and margin), and bar chart elements for monthly shipment counts. The skill handles edge cases including Qlik Sense loading delays, session persistence across invocations, and detection of excluded months without data. Output is formatted into three standardized tables showing principal KPIs, air/maritime mix, and traffic counts, providing Roy with a consistent, repeatable interface for retrieving monthly logistics and financial performance data.</narrative>
